title: ceropegia quadrangula
intro: Ceropegia quadrangula, also known as the four-angled milk vine, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in various cultures. Ceropegia quadrangula can be prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, and is often used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
